sections.paragraph:
φιλόστοργος, ον (s. prec. entry; X.+; ins [e.g. SEG XLII, 1214, 12: II a.d.; cp. adv. ISmyrnaMcCabe 9, 6: 245/243 b.c.]; PMich 148 II, 9 [I a.d.]; for other ins and pap s. New Docs 3, 41f; 4 Macc 15:13; TestAbr A 1 p. 78, 4 [Stone p. 4]; JosAs 12:8; Philo; Jos., Ant. 7, 252 al.) loving dearly τῇ φιλαδελφίᾳ εἰς ἀλλήλους φιλόστοργοι devoted to one another in brotherly love Ro 12:10.—DELG s.v. στέργω. M-M. Spicq.

prec. prec. = preceding

  1. X. = Xenophon, V–IV b.c.—List 5

ins Ins, ins = Inscription, Inschrift, inscription(s). Without a period, esp. in lists, as at the beginning of entries; the capitalized form is used in titles. In conjunction with literary works this abbr. refers to the title or description of contents.

e.g. e.g. = exempli gratia (for example)

SEG SEG = Supplementum Epigraphicum Graecum—List 3

  1. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts

adv. adv. = adverb, adverbially

ISmyrnaMcCabe ISmyrnaMcCabe = Smyrna Inscriptions, Texts and List, prepared by DMcCabe et al.—List 3

PMich PMich = Michigan Papyri—List 4

pap pap = papyrus, -yri

TestAbr TestAbr = Testament of Abraham, with some interpolations; I b.c./I a.d.—Lists 2, 5

JosAs JosAs = Joseph and Aseneth—Lists 2, 5

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

  1. al. =alibi (elsewhere), aliter (otherwise), alii (others)

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

Spicq Spicq = CS., Lexique théologique du Nouveau Testament—Lists 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 1059
